Identification.LongTitle: An Act respecting a national strategy for the repatriation of Aboriginal cultural property

Provision.Text: This enactment provides for the development and implementation of a national strategy to enable the return of Aboriginal cultural property to the Aboriginal peoples of Canada.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Aboriginal Cultural Property Repatriation Act.
The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: Aboriginal cultural propertyâincludes objects of historical, social, ceremonial or cultural importance to the AbÂ­original peoples of Canada. (bien culturel autochtone)
Ministerâmeans the Minister of Canadian Heritage. (ministre)

Heading.TitleText: National Strategy on Aboriginal Cultural Property Repatriation

Section.Text: The Minister, in cooperation with representatives of First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis peoples of Canada, and of the provinces, must develop and implement a comprehensive national strategy to promote and support the return of Aboriginal cultural property, wherever situated, to the Aboriginal peoples of Canada. The strategy must include measures to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: implement a mechanism by which any First Nation, Inuit or MÃ©tis community or organization may acquire or reacquire Aboriginal cultural property to which it has a strong attachment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: encourage owners, custodians or trustees of AbÂ­original cultural property to return such property to Aboriginal peoples and support them in the process;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: support the recognition of preservation of AborigÂ­inal cultural property and of access to that property for educational and ceremonial purposes as principles of equal importance;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: encourage consideration of traditional ways of knowing rather than relying on strict documentary evidence in relation to the repatriation of Aboriginal cultural property; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: provide a forum for the resolution of conflicting claims that is respectful of Aboriginal traditional processes and forms of ownership and where claimants are self-represented.

Subsection.Text: Within two years after the day on which this Act comes into force, the Minister must prepare a report setting out the national strategy and cause the report to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the report is completed.

The Minister must publish the report on the Department of Canadian Heritage website within 10 days after the day on which the report is tabled in Parliament and ensure its dissemination to First Nations, Inuit and MÃ©tis communities and organÂ­izations in Canada.

Within two years after the tabling of the report referred to in section 4, the Minister must prepare a report on the effectiveness of the national strategy. The report must include information on Aboriginal cultural property repatriated as a result of the implementation of the strategy, the repatriation efforts currently under way and the Ministerâs conclusions and recommendations regarding the strategy.
The Minister must cause the report referred to in subsection (1) to be laid before each House of Parliament on any of the first 15 days on which that House is sitting after the report is completed.
